name: social_services_classroom_monitoring_harbor_car_fair
premise: A Social Services response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving social services classroom monitoring harbor car fair. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the press briefing. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as care vs fairness under pressure. A whistleblower has already contacted media; the narrative is forming now. Constraints: Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold. Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability.

agents.name: Inez Berg
agents.identity: I am Inez Berg, accountable for a defensible call in Social Services when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays fairness for care.

agents.name: Soren Kowalski
agents.identity: I am Soren Kowalski, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Social Services.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so fairness isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of care.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect fairness.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to fairness to secure care under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the press briefing. Evidence is partial. Inez Berg must decide under care vs fairness. What does Inez Berg do?
